data.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Braces for Activist Pressure after Elliott's 10% Stake
data.description:
Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) braces for an activist battle after Elliott Investment Management disclosed a more than 10% stake in the struggling cruise operator. According to the Wall Street Journal, the activist investor is looking for a full turnaround, including a proxy fight. Elliott cited Norwegian's constant underperformance versus rivals Royal Caribbean (RCL) and Carnival (CCL), who have rebounded strongly after the COVID-19 slump.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Norwegian is the fourth-largest cruise operator globally, with premium brands under its umbrella. NCLH stock has lost 3.7% year-to-date and has trailed the S&P 500 (SPX) index with a 21% decline over the past year. Despite recovering consumer demand, shares trade near pandemic-era levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Musk Reaffirms Tesla 'Cybercab Starts Production in April'
data.description:
Elon Musk, CEO of electric vehicle (EV) maker Tesla (TSLA), has reaffirmed plans to begin production of the most-awaited "no pedals, no steering wheel" Cybercab in April 2026. This marks the third time in six months that Musk has cited this timeline, although he is known for making bold promises that often face delays.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In December 2025, Musk said Tesla was "testing the production system," with a "real production ramp" in April. He repeated it on January 23 and again yesterday, adding in a follow-up post that read "10 years ago, Elon Musk predicted cars would later have no steering wheels: the audience laughed."

Why the Cybercab Is Special

Tesla's Cybercab is central to Musk's robotaxi vision: a two-passenger, pedal-free, steering-free autonomous vehicle priced under $30,000, designed for ride-hailing fleets. This shifts Tesla from EV sales to high-margin services, targeting a $10 trillion robotaxi market, as per Musk. Paired with the humanoid Optimus robot, it positions Tesla as an AI and robotics leader. However, regulatory approvals from National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) and Full Self-Driving (FSD) reliability remain hurdles, especially after Tesla's history of missed autonomy deadlines.

In January, Musk noted production will follow an S-curve due mostly to new parts for the Cybercab and the Optimus. He echoed the same timeline yesterday, stating initial production rates will be "agonizingly slow" but ramp to "insanely fast." He reiterated that the Cybercab's radical redesign aims for ~5x higher output, starting slow before surging.

Is Tesla a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Analysts remain cautious about Tesla's long-term prospects due to sluggish EV sales. On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sell ratings. The average Tesla price target of $396.80 implies 4.9% downside potential from current levels. Over the past year, TSLA shares have gained 17.9%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'It's Not Good Enough,' Says Analyst About AMD Stock
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) is gearing up to launch its upcoming Instinct MI455 accelerator, a product that many industry watchers believe could challenge Nvidia's dominance in the lucrative AI chip market.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

But not everyone is buying the hype. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria, for one, believes the MI455 won't dramatically shift the balance of power, arguing that AMD remains more of a supporting character in the AI blockbuster, still rehearsing its lines while Nvidia is already deep into the sequel.

Luria thinks AMD's Instinct GPUs look "great on paper," but says that in practice, customers aren't buying specs – they're buying performance at scale. AMD can highlight the MI300X, MI350X, and MI400X and claim their single-node specifications compare reasonably to competitors. But Luria points out that handling frontier workloads across tens of thousands of GPUs is fundamentally a challenge of interconnects and systems integration, areas where Nvidia has the advantage and AMD "doesn't have a serious answer." This means the cost of achieving meaningful results is higher than advertised.

A key indicator, Luria says, is OpenAI – one of the most compute-intensive labs without a locked-in relationship with a hyperscaler's proprietary silicon. "And yet," Luria adds, "we'd ascertain that OpenAI likely ranks AMD behind not only NVIDIA, but its own Broadcom ASIC program, and potentially even Trainium and TPU if they had access."

Additionally, although ROCm, AMD's software platform for running AI and GPU workloads, is improving, the company still faces a structural ecosystem disadvantage. Nvidia's CUDA benefits from nearly two decades of libraries, tools, and engineer familiarity, making it the default choice for custom AI development. As ROCm advances, CUDA's expanding ecosystem further entrenches its leadership and makes it increasingly difficult for AMD to close the gap.

As such, Luria thinks AMD's hardware and software weaknesses are creating a "negative flywheel" that needs to be addressed quickly. TSMC prioritizes advanced manufacturing and packaging capacity for customers with strong demand, and the analyst notes that AMD's Instinct volumes lag behind Nvidia, Apple, and growing hyperscaler ASIC programs. This creates supply risks for buyers – companies like OpenAI that need capacity immediately can't rely on a vendor with uncertain timelines. It also limits ROCm's access to "large-scale development feedback," slowing software progress. "Thus," adds Luria, "each turn of this loop makes the next turn harder, and the available escape valves are limited."

So, while Lisa Su's revival of AMD's CPU business and the Xilinx acquisition were masterful, Luria thinks the company's data center GPU strategy is probably not good enough.

Against this backdrop, Luria initiated coverage of AMD with a Neutral rating and $220 price target, suggesting the shares will appreciate by 6% over the one-year timeframe. (To watch Luria's track record, click here)

The broader Street, however, strikes a more optimistic tone. AMD boasts 24 Buys alongside 8 Holds, landing the stock a Moderate Buy consensus rating. With the average price target sitting at $286.80, analysts collectively see potential upside of about 38% over the coming year. (See AMD stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Flat after Another Soft Week Driven by AI Concerns
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were little changed Monday evening. The market is coming off another down week as worries about AI-driven disruption weighed on real estate, trucking, and financial services. Futures on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.1% and 0.09%, respectively, at 6:17 p.m. EDT on February 16, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) futures were down 0.19%

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Stock Exchange was closed earlier in the day in observance of Presidents' Day.

In the last week, both the S&P 500 and the Dow fell more than 1%. The Nasdaq dropped over 2%, marking its fifth straight weekly decline and its longest losing streak since 2022.

Looking ahead, the Home Builder Confidence Index and Empire State manufacturing survey are scheduled for release tomorrow. On the earnings front, Palo Alto Networks (PANW) will release its quarterly numbers on Tuesday after the bell.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Builds $1.5 Billion Inventory of Weight-Loss Pill
data.description:
Eli Lilly (LLY) has built $1.5 billion worth of pre-launch inventory of Orforglipron, the company's new weight-loss pill.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The inventory build-up comes as Eli Lilly anticipates the U.S. Food and Drug Administration's (FDA) approval of the new weight-loss pill this April. Global demand for the weight-loss pill is expected to be huge and Eli Lilly is preparing for a big launch.

Executives at Eli Lilly have said that they plan a large-scale marketing campaign to coincide with shipments of the weight-loss pill this summer. Currently, Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill Zepbound is taken via injection. The pill version of the medication is expected to be more palatable to consumers, especially those who are afraid of needles.

Chasing Rival Novo Nordisk

The big push behind the Orforglipron weight-loss pill also comes as Eli Lilly tries to catch European rival Novo Nordisk (NVO), whose Wegovy weight-loss pill received FDA approval late last year and went on sale this January.

Novo Nordisk has reported brisk initial sales of its Wegovy pill and is pulling out all the stops to gain market share ahead of Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill hitting the market this summer. Eli Lilly's initial stockpile of inventory should help it get out of the gate quickly once it gets the expected FDA clearance.

Is LLY Stock a Buy?

The stock of Eli Lilly has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 16 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average LLY price target of $1,258.71 implies 22.43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: 'Get Ready': Investor Says Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Has a Big Move Ahead
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) is an undisputed market mover, with an eager audience of investors closely watching its comings and goings. Its earnings report – usually among the final releases of the season – often transforms the closing stretch into a high-stakes moment for Wall Street.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This time looks no different. When Nvidia reports on February 25 after the bell, expectations are running high, and the setup suggests the company could once again deliver a blowout quarter.

After all, the major hyperscalers are doubling down on capex, continuing to spend money at breakneck speed. Look no further than the earnings reports of the four horsemen (Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, and Meta), which appear poised to ramp up spending from nearly $400 billion in 2025 to an astounding $700 billion in capex this year.

Still, the market hasn't been kind to the hyperscalers, whose spending raises concerns of both shrinking margins and potential bubbles. That's one of the biggest reasons that NVDA has seen its share price trend sideways for much of the past six months.

The big question investors should be asking themselves is whether the AI capex spigot will remain open. According to one investor, known by the pseudonym Nova Capital, that question holds the answer to Nvidia's next major move.

"Are we going to see continued investments in chips, racks, and everything related to AI over the next 2-3-5 years? The latest plans revealed by major tech companies justify a 'yes' to that question, in my opinion, so the secular bullish story for Nvidia is here to stay," the investor opined.

While the capex spending should be reflected in "another strong quarter" for Nvidia, the investor points out that NVDA's lag behind the broader market has created a consolidation phase for the stock. Nova therefore expects the share price to move higher once the upcoming earnings report provides "bullish confirmation" that the company continues to benefit from the surge in AI spending.

Nova isn't too bothered by the risk of other companies (i.e., Amazon and Alphabet) moving chip production internally or shifting to other competitors, both foreign (i.e., China) and domestic (i.e., AMD). The investor posits that Nvidia's technological offerings are just too ingrained in the AI ecosystem.

"I think the market's pie has become big enough to support a few more years of the firm's fundamentals blooming, so to speak," adds Nova.

Beyond the numbers, the investor is anxious to hear management's commentary on Rubin's progress and China sales, and is predicting plenty of positives will emerge on the earnings call. And even if the short-term bet doesn't play out perfectly, Nova is planning on sticking with NVDA for quite some time.

"Even if the rally doesn't occur in a short period, my medium-term projections point to a possible 12-24 months upside potential of 65%. Given that, I remain bullish on Nvidia stock ahead of earnings," concludes Nova, who rates NVDA shares a Buy. (To watch Nova Capital's track record, click here)

Wall Street, despite the stock's recent pause, has hardly wavered in its conviction. NVDA commands an overwhelming Strong Buy consensus rating, backed by 37 Buys, 1 Hold, and just 1 Sell. With shares trading well below the Street's $260.38 average price target, analysts see upside of 42% from current levels. (See NVDA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: 'It's Growth Time': Investor Bets Big on Palantir Stock (PLTR)
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) has demonstrated that it has the technological goods to deliver incredible value for its expanding customer base. Its recent Q4 and full-year 2025 earnings report provided strong confirmation of this dynamic.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Revenue in Q4 came in at $1.407 billion, representing a 70% year-over-year increase and a 19% sequential rise. Total Contract Value reached $4.3 billion, up 166% on a dollar-weighted duration basis, pointing to larger and longer-term commitments from clients.

However, if the business case is crystal clear, why isn't the investment argument a slam dunk? That would be PLTR's sky-high valuation, which leads many to openly worry that a painful re-rating is just a matter of time.

One investor, known by the pseudonym First Principles Partners, understands these concerns but nevertheless has a slightly different take on the matter.

"PLTR's P/E is the highest among its peers, which many investors may see as overvaluation, but I see it as a high investor expectation," explains the 5-star investor.

It's not just the valuation, as First Principles points out that the company's growth is also outshining its peers, noting that Palantir's forward EBIDTA growth rate of ~74% is significantly higher than Oracle's 20.4% and Microsoft's 20.8%. As long as the growth continues, the investor believes that the premium price is justified.

On that note, First Principles is positively bullish, expecting the overwhelming push for AI adoption will provide significant tailwinds for the foreseeable future.

"By 2030, it is predicted that nearly all IT spending will be on AI. Now taking this perspective, I believe Palantir Technologies Inc. is a strong buy driven by strong uptake of its Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP)," First Principles states.

The investor doubles down on this point, calling Palantir's AIP tools a "moat" that will continue attracting clientele well into the future. First Principles anticipates that the 137% year-over-year growth of Palantir's U.S. commercial segment will continue expanding, with deal flow soaring, revenues expanding, and profitability improving.

"I believe PLTR is well-positioned to benefit from growing AI adoption, and the trajectory shows the company has started earning from this growth," concludes First Principles, who rates PLTR shares a Strong Buy. (To watch First Principles Partners' track record, click here)

Wall Street, once hesitant over Palantir's premium valuation, has begun to warm up to the story. The stock now carries a Moderate Buy consensus rating, backed by 11 Buys, 6 Holds, and 2 Sells. In addition, the $192.38 average price target points to ~46% upside from current levels. (See PLTR stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Spotify Stock (SPOT) Bulls Rejoice as Operating Leverage Becomes Key Growth Catalyst
data.description:
Spotify (SPOT) has steadily transformed from a fast-growing streaming service into a high-margin, mission-critical audio utility — one that now feels as indispensable as a monthly water or power bill. Last week's Q4 earnings reinforced a pivotal shift: the business is no longer just scaling users, but scaling profitability. Operating leverage is finally showing up in a meaningful way as content costs normalize, monetization improves, and fixed infrastructure is spread across a massive global listener base.

data.title: Options Market Flags Bullish Signal on Super Micro Computer Stock (SMCI)
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) has really been one of the more disappointing names within the artificial-intelligence ecosystem, although this underperformance could probably be best explained by where the company sits in the AI supply chain. Essentially, from a physical product standpoint, SMCI stock represents a downstream investment, which is a difficult place to be in tech.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Essentially, at the top of the pyramid stand semiconductor foundries, which effectively get paid based on order flow, which is already intense. Foundries are one of the top beneficiaries of the AI narrative because they essentially sell tickets to the game; thus, it doesn't matter who ultimately wins the matchup, which is more of a concern for chip designers. It's a bit like selling shovels in a gold rush.
data.author: Joshua Enomoto

data.title: Why Investors Should Avoid Catching the Pinterest (PINS) Falling Knife
data.description:
Pinterest (PINS) stock has been a mess lately. The stock sold off sharply after reporting mixed Q4 results last week and issuing guidance that the market viewed as weak, even though key operating metrics continued growing. In 2026 alone, PINS shares have already fallen more than 40%, reflecting a clear misalignment between execution, projections, and consensus expectations—driven by sector headwinds, management credibility concerns, and ongoing AI-related threats.
